5. More Group Theory

5.1. Group Actions

  • Def Given group and finite set , a group action of on is a map satisfying

    1. (Identity)
    2. (Compatilibity)
    • Ex If a group acts by left or right multiplication, we must have and , respectively.
    • Prop (Equivalent Definition) For a given , a group action defines a homomorphism . Conversely, if is a homomorphism, we can define a group action.

    (30)

    • Prop Stabilizers in the same orbit are isomorphic. In particular, let belong to the same orbit, where . Then

  • Classifying actions

    • Def An action is transitive if there is only one orbit.

      • By orbit-stabilizer, must be a multiple of .

    • Def An action is faithful if each non-identity element acts non-trivially on , i.e. the homomorphism is injective.

      • Equivalent definition: the intersection of all the stabilizers is identity.

    • Def An action is free when there are no nontrivial stabilizers.

      • Then each orbit has size .
      • Ex. acts freely on .

    • Ex Group action acting on a set of 7 elements.

      • By orbit-stabilizer, the orbits must have size dividing , so the orbits must have size , , or . acts transitively on an orbit, so
      • Such action cannot be transitive; 15 is not a multiple of 7. It cannot be faithful, there is no subgroup of order 15 in . It cannot be free,

    • Prop If a group has a subgroup of index , then there exists a homomorphism from to .

      • Ex There does not exist a subgroup of of order , since a homomorphism from to would have nontrivial kernel but is simple.

5.5. Simplicity of the alternating group

  • Def A group is simple if it has no nontrivial normal subgroups other than itself.

    • Ex is simple. A nontrivial normal subgroup is the union of conjugacy classes including . No such union has a size that divides .
    • Ex is not simple. The Klein four group inside it is normal.

  • Lem 1. For , acts transitively on the set of ordered pairs .
    Proof. For any , the permutation does the job.

    • Lem 2. For , the centralizer of in is trivial. In other words, there is no element in that commutes with every element of .

    Proof. Suppose commutes with . Then for all we have . I claim that for all . Suppose there is an such that . Then there exists such that and . does not commute with and we have a contradiction. □

  • Thm is simple for .
    Proof. Proceed by induction on . Suppose is a normal subgroup of , take any . First, suppose has a fixed point. WLOG since is normal we can set . Then can be embedded into . By the induction hypothesis, . So contains all the three cycles and equals . Suppose there is no with a fixed point. WLOG we can have . By Lem 2 there exists a nontrivial such that , so . But , so this reduces to the first case. □

5.7. Sylow Theorems

  • Def Suppose is a finite group of order and , i.e. is the largest power of prime that divides . is a -Sylow subgroup if .

  • Prop Let be the set of subsets of of size . acts freely on via left multiplication.

    • Lem 1. Let be a subset of . Then divides both and .

  • Lem 2. choose is congruent to .

    Proof.

    (42)

  • Thm (Sylow 1) Every group has a -Sylow subgroup for all primes dividing .

    Proof. Consider the action of on the set of subsets of size . Since the orbits partition , . By Lem 2, is not divisible by , so there must exist such that is not divisible by . By Lem 1, divides , so it must equal . Thus is a -Sylow subgroup. □

    • Cor (Cauchy) If is divisible by prime then contains an element of order .

      Pf. Let be a -Sylow subgroup of , and take any nontrivial so that for . Then has order .

  • Thm (Sylow 2) The follow two statements hold:

    1. Let be a subgroup of order for some . Then there exists a Sylow subgroup containing . In other words, every -subgroup is contained in a -Sylow subgroup.
    2. Any two -Sylyow subgroups of are conjugate and th us isomorphic.

    Proof. Fix a -Sylow subgroup . To prove both statements, it suffices to show that for all with order , there exists such that . Consider acting on via left multiplication. Since , by the fixed point theorem there exists a coset that is fixed by . [TODO] The stabilizer of when acts on is . It contains . □

  • Thm (Sylow 3) The number of -Sylow subgroups divides and equals .

    Proof. Consider acting on its set of subgroups by conjugation. Then the -Sylow subgroups form a single orbit by Sylow 2. Let be a -Sylow subgroup. The normalizer of is the set of such that . The is a subgroup of so the order is divisble by . By orbit-stabilizer, the orbit must divide .

5.8. Groups of order

Thm Groups of order where and are prime have one of the following structures:

  2. If , is cyclic or has a unique nonabelian structure:

    (43)

    where and .

Proof.
Let and be - and -Sylow groups of , respectively. and are both cyclic since they have prime order, and because the nontrivial elements in and have different orders. There can only be -Sylow subgroup, implying is normal. By the proposition on product of subgroups, is a subgroup of , and there exists a biijection between and . Thus , implying and there exists a bijection between and .

In case 1, there can only be -Sylow subgroup and -Sylow subgroup, implying they are both normal and that the product map is an isomorphism. Thus .
